causal_chain: Solar array current I_sa dropped to 0.0A at T-300s (expected 8.5A)
Battery voltage V_bat fell to 21.8V (below 28V lower limit)
State of Charge dropped to 14.2% triggering load shedding
FDIR entered safe mode via EPS_UNDER_VOLT flag
Possible cascade if thermal conditions caused solar cell degradation
OBC_temp_C at 18.2°C is nominal — no thermal evidence
Communications loss could mask ground recovery commands
No comms anomaly visible in telemetry or event log

recovery_plan.rationale: Query current battery voltage, SoC, and solar array status before recovery
recovery_plan.verify: Battery and solar array readings received successfully

recovery_plan.rationale: Reduce power draw to preserve remaining battery capacity
recovery_plan.verify: Non-essential subsystems (PYLD, heaters) powered off; V_bus stable

recovery_plan.rationale: Cycle solar array relay to potentially restore connection
recovery_plan.verify: I_sa > 2.0A indicating partial or full array reconnection

recovery_plan.rationale: Re-orient spacecraft for maximum solar illumination if array relay succeeded

sentinel_output.reasoning_summary: Solar array A complete current loss at T-300s caused progressive battery depletion. V_bat dropped to 21.8V and SoC to 14.2%. Load shedding was initiated but insufficient to prevent safe-mode entry. Recovery: status check → load shed → relay cycle → sun-point.
